title: Polypodium biforme
intro: Polypodium biforme, also known as the two-formed polypody or southern polypody, is a species of fern native to the southeastern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium biforme include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Polypodium biforme is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or salve, often used topically to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and internally to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Polypodium biforme is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a member of the genus Polypodium, which comprises about 80 species of terrestrial ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is part of the order Polypodiales, a large group of ferns that also includes the families Dryopteridaceae and Athyriaceae. Polypodium biforme is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Polypodium biforme reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns in the Polypodiales clade.
uses: Polypodium biforme is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues.
